description: Trigger-and-behaviour scenarios for the MCP debugging skill. Each prompt is something a user would actually say; assertions describe what a good response should surface. Run the skill against a fresh agent and grade the response against the assertions.

evals.prompt: I added a filesystem MCP server to my claude_desktop_config.json on Windows 11 but it never shows up in the connectors menu. The log says 'spawn npx ENOENT'. My config has "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "C:\\Users\\me\\data"].
evals.expected_output: Identifies the Windows .cmd-shim spawn failure and recommends wrapping with cmd /c (command=cmd, args=[/c, npx, -y, ...]) or pointing at an absolute node.exe, explicitly noting that an absolute npx.cmd path is NOT a fix on patched Node. Mentions running the diagnostics script and a full quit/reopen.
assertions: Recommends the cmd /c wrapper (command=cmd, args starting with /c) as the fix
States that an absolute path to npx.cmd does not fix it (EINVAL on patched Node)
Does not merely say 'use an absolute executable path' as the sole fix
Mentions full quit/reopen and/or the diagnostics script

evals.prompt: My MCP server connects in Claude Desktop and then immediately disconnects. It works fine when I run it from the terminal. It's a Node server.
evals.expected_output: Directs the user to the per-server stderr log (mcp-server-<NAME>.log, distinct from mcp.log), and prioritizes the stdout-contamination test (any non-JSON on stdout breaks framing; use console.error not console.log). Mentions env/cwd divergence as the 'works in terminal' clue and the stdout-only pipe test.
assertions: Names mcp-server-<NAME>.log (per-server stderr) as the file to read, distinct from mcp.log
Raises stdout/JSON-RPC contamination (console.log to stdout) as a leading cause
Gives the stdout-only contamination test (e.g. node server.js 2>/dev/null | head)

evals.prompt: Our remote MCP server worked, but now every request returns HTTP 404 after the connection sits idle for a while. Re-running OAuth doesn't help.
evals.expected_output: Distinguishes a transport session expiry from an auth problem: 404 carrying an old Mcp-Session-Id means the session is gone; the client must reinitialize with a fresh InitializeRequest and NO session header. Notes this is a known client/SDK bug class and that re-auth is the wrong fix.
assertions: Identifies 404 as an expired/terminated transport session, not an auth failure
Says to reinitialize without the old Mcp-Session-Id
Explicitly separates session expiry from OAuth/token expiry
Does not recommend re-running OAuth as the fix

evals.prompt: I'm connecting Claude to a remote MCP server at https://mcp.acme.com/mcp and it keeps failing auth in a loop. How do I figure out what's wrong with the OAuth?
evals.expected_output: Walks the OAuth discovery chain with concrete evidence-gathering: send an unauthenticated request and read WWW-Authenticate for resource_metadata; GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource for authorization_servers; GET the AS metadata; check audience/resource binding and DCR. Points at the mcp_http_probe.py script or curl, and flags a 401 with no WWW-Authenticate as the classic loop cause.
assertions: Describes the 401 -> WWW-Authenticate -> Protected Resource Metadata -> authorization server discovery chain
Suggests probing with the bundled script (mcp_http_probe.py) or curl against the endpoint and .well-known paths
Mentions audience/resource binding, DCR, or PKCE as specific failure points
Flags a 401 missing the WWW-Authenticate/resource_metadata pointer as a top cause

evals.prompt: I put my MCP server config in VS Code under mcpServers in mcp.json but the tools never appear in agent mode. Same JSON works in Cursor.
evals.expected_output: Identifies that VS Code uses the top-level key 'servers' (not 'mcpServers') and requires a 'type' field per entry, unlike Cursor/Claude Desktop. Points to the per-client config-key cheat-sheet and the VS Code MCP output/trust commands.
assertions: States VS Code uses 'servers' (not 'mcpServers') as the top-level key
Notes VS Code requires a 'type' field on each server entry
Explains why the same JSON works in Cursor (which uses mcpServers)
